article_title: Late-onset Kinking of the Ascenda Catheter following Intrathecal Baclofen Pump Implantation: A Case Report
label: Fig. 1
caption: A) ITB pump implantation was performed 5 years of age. Three-dimensional CT imaging demonstrates an Ascenda catheter with its tip positioned at the C7 vertebral level (yellow arrow head). B) At the age of 10 years, as the patient grew taller, the catheter tip migrated caudally to the T6 vertebral level (yellow arrowheads), and slight scoliosis was observed on radiography. C) At the age of 14 years, the catheter tip migrated caudally to the T8 vertebral level (yellow arrowheads), and progression of scoliosis over the past four years was noted on radiography. CT: computed tomography; ITB: Intrathecal baclofen

article_title: Late-onset Kinking of the Ascenda Catheter following Intrathecal Baclofen Pump Implantation: A Case Report
label: Fig. 2
caption: A) Kinking of the catheter was observed just proximal to the anchor on three dimensional CT (yellow arrow). B) Intraoperatively, a looped and kinked segment of the previously implanted Ascenda catheter was identified just proximal to the anchor with dense adhesions to the surrounding connective tissue. C) The kinked segment of the previous catheter is removed and cut (yellow arrow), and a new catheter is connected. CT: computed tomography
